answer:
We have \( R = 4\,\Omega \), \( L = 4\,\text{H} \), and \( C = 1/2\,\text{F} \).  
The characteristic equation is \( L s^2 + R s + \frac{1}{C} = 4s^2 + 4s + 2 = 0 \), which simplifies to \( s^2 + s + 0.5 = 0 \).  
The roots are \( s = -0.5 \pm j0.5 \), so the homogeneous solution has the form \( e^{-t/2} \left( A\cos(t/2) + B\sin(t/2) \right) \), matching option J.

\boxed{J}
